event: 66 years old
male
with a history of dual-chamber pacemaker
implanted due to sick sinus syndrome
and a history of paroxysmal atrial fibrillation
presented with complaints of severe lumbar back pain
deteriorated rapidly with mental status changes
hypotension
acute renal failure
hypoxemic respiratory failure with emergent intubation
diagnosed with severe sepsis
blood cultures on admission yielded methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus
diagnosed with positive bubble study, demonstrating the presence of a patent foramen ovale
Doppler of the intra-atrial septum demonstrated that a medium-sized patent foramen ovale measuring approximately 2.99 cm was present on color Doppler without Valsalva maneuver
computed tomography scan of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis revealed osteomyelitis of the lower spine in the region of L2–L3
magnetic resonance imaging of the brain demonstrated multiple small, 2- to 3-mm, acute infarctions in multiple vascular distributions
septic emboli to the distal extremities
repeat blood cultures revealed the same organism for 3 consecutive days
transesophageal echocardiogram (TEE) was recommended
demonstrated a long (0.26 × 3.6 cm), independently mobile structure within the right atrium (RA), consistent with a vegetation
the structure appeared to be attached to the pacemaker lead just above the tricuspid valve
a second mobile structure attached to the pacemaker lead within the right ventricle
no evidence of aortic and/or mitral valve endocarditis, or of aortic arch infected atheromata
cardiothoracic surgery was consulted for the possibility of surgical intervention
patient was deemed a poor surgical candidate due to hemodynamic instability
a decision was made to perform a laser lead extraction procedure
laser lead extraction procedure was performed
the patient arrived in the hybrid operating room intubated and sedated
bilateral femoral veins were accessed using the modified Seldinger technique
a 16F GORE® DrySeal Flex introducer sheath with hydrophilic coating was placed in the right common femoral vein
an 8F sheath was inserted into the left common femoral vein
a 9F sheath was placed in the right femoral vein
an 8F catheter (CARTO SoundStar; Biosense Webster, Irvine, CA) was advanced from the right femoral vein into the RA
a 91.4-cm 12F 45-degree curved sheath (Cook Medical, Bloomington, IN) was advanced into the RA with a long 0.035 wire
a 115-cm Indigo CAT8 XTORQ with a 45-degree curve catheter was then advanced through the 12F guiding sheath into the RA
intracardiac echocardiography (ICE) was used to obtain precise slices of the RV lead vegetations in view
the vegetation was attached to the RV lead with the stalk at the level of the tricuspid valve
the aspiration was accomplished by connecting the perfusion catheter to the aspiration pump
performed 2 aspiration passes with 100 mL drawn with each pass
during the first pass, blood in the vacuum container was strained and revealed the presence of the smaller vegetation in the container
a second pass was performed closer to the body of the vegetation
the Indigo Penumbra system was withdrawn into the inferior vena cava to allow us to proceed with the lead extraction
using well-described procedural steps for laser lead extraction, we successfully extracted the RA and RV leads
the pocket was carefully inspected and closed with suture
the Penumbra and ICE systems were removed from the body and hemostasis was achieved
the total procedure time was 72 minutes and the blood loss was 200 mL
both vegetations were sent to pathology for analysis and aerobic cultures
pathology of the vegetation specimens showed via gram staining that there were gram-positive cocci in chains
culture-positive for methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus
the patient's condition improved for the first 48 hours after the procedure
after 72 hours of stability, the patient's condition took a turn for the worse
tissue necrosis of the distal upper and lower extremities
lactic acidosis
worsening renal failure
given a grim prognosis, the family decided to withdraw care
the patient was extubated to comfort care and died
